modules.title: Module 1: Foundation
modules.description: Learn the basics of AI and how it works

content.heading: What is Artificial Intelligence?
content.body:
**AI (Artificial Intelligence)** is technology that enables computers to learn, understand, and make decisions like humans.

**Real-world examples:**
- Google Search results → AI
- YouTube/Netflix recommendations → AI
- ChatGPT, Claude conversations → AI
- Voice assistants (Siri, Alexa) → AI

**How does AI work?**
AI learns patterns from massive amounts of data. Just like you learned to recognize a cat by seeing thousands of cat pictures, AI learns the same way.
content.note: 💡 Remember: AI isn't magic — it's math and data working together.

exercise.title: Try it yourself
exercise.description: Ask the AI to explain itself in simple terms:
exercise.defaultPrompt: Explain what you are and how you work, in simple terms for a complete beginner.

quiz.question: What does AI stand for?
How does AI primarily learn?
Which of these is an example of AI?

content.heading: Large Language Models (LLMs)
content.body:
**LLM (Large Language Model)** is a type of AI trained on massive amounts of text to understand and generate human language.

**Popular LLMs:**
- **GPT (OpenAI)** — powers ChatGPT
- **Claude (Anthropic)** — known for safety and reasoning
- **Gemini (Google)** — integrated with Google services
- **Llama (Meta)** — open source model

**What makes them 'Large'?**
They're trained on billions of words from books, websites, and articles — giving them broad knowledge across countless topics.

**How do they generate text?**
LLMs predict the next word based on context — doing this billions of times per second creates human-like responses.
content.note: 💡 LLMs predict the next word based on patterns — but they do it so well it feels like real understanding.

exercise.title: Try it yourself
exercise.description: Ask about the differences between LLMs:
exercise.defaultPrompt: What's the difference between GPT, Claude, and Gemini? Explain in a simple comparison table.

quiz.question: What does LLM stand for?
Which company created Claude?
LLMs are trained on:

content.heading: Prompt — Your Instructions to AI
content.body:
A **prompt** is what you write or say to an AI to get a response.

Just like a good recipe leads to good food, a good prompt leads to a good AI response.

**A good prompt includes:**
- What you want (the task)
- Who it's for (role/context)
- How you want it (format)
- Any constraints (length, tone)

examples.label: Strong Prompt
examples.text: I'm a beginner cook. Give me a simple 5-minute fried egg recipe with step-by-step instructions and exact measurements.

content.note: 💡 The more specific your prompt, the more accurate the response.

exercise.title: Try it yourself
exercise.description: Improve this weak prompt and send it:
exercise.defaultPrompt: Give me a recipe

quiz.question: What is a prompt?
Which prompt is more effective?

content.heading: Tokens & Context Window Explained
content.body:
**What is a Token?**
AI doesn't read word by word — it reads in chunks called tokens. 1 token ≈ 4 characters or ¾ of a word.

**Examples:**
- 'Hello' = 1 token
- 'PromptForge' = 2-3 tokens
- 100 words ≈ 75 tokens

**What is a Context Window?**
The context window is how much text an AI can 'see' at once — like its short-term memory. If your conversation is too long, the AI may forget earlier parts.

**Why does this matter?**
- Longer prompts cost more tokens
- Very long conversations may lose context
- Keep your prompts clear and concise
content.note: 💡 Think of context window as the AI's working memory — it can only hold so much at once.

exercise.title: Try it yourself
exercise.description: Ask about tokens in your own words:
exercise.defaultPrompt: Explain what tokens are in AI language models. Give a simple analogy to help me understand.

quiz.question: Approximately how many tokens is 100 words?
What is a context window?
options: How much text AI can process at once

content.heading: Real-World AI Use Cases
content.body:
AI can help you with an enormous range of tasks. Here's what's possible:

**Writing & Content:**
- Drafting emails, essays, reports
- Writing code and debugging
- Creating marketing copy

**Research & Learning:**
- Summarizing long documents
- Explaining complex topics
- Answering questions instantly

**Creative Work:**
- Brainstorming ideas
- Writing stories or scripts
- Generating images (with image AI)

**Business & Productivity:**
- Analyzing data
- Creating business plans
- Automating repetitive tasks

**What AI cannot do:**
- Access real-time internet (usually)
- Guarantee 100% accuracy
- Replace human judgment entirely
content.note: 💡 AI is a powerful tool — but it works best when you know exactly what to ask for.

exercise.title: Try it yourself
exercise.description: Ask AI to help with something from your real life:
exercise.defaultPrompt: I'm a student preparing for exams. Give me 5 creative ways I can use AI to study more effectively.

quiz.question: Which of these can AI help with?
options: Writing, research, creativity, and business tasks

quiz.question: What is a limitation of most AI chatbots?
options: They don't have real-time internet access

modules.title: Module 2: Prompt Basics
modules.description: Master the fundamentals of effective prompting

content.heading: Specificity — The Key to Better Prompts
content.body:
AI can't read your mind. Whatever you say, that's exactly what it understands.

**Add these elements to be specific:**

**1. Quantity**
'Give some tips' → 'Give exactly 5 tips'

**2. Audience**
'Explain it' → 'Explain it to a 10-year-old'

**3. Format**
'Write it' → 'Write it as numbered bullet points'

**4. Length**
'Briefly' or 'In detail' — AI doesn't know how much you need.

**5. Tone**
'Professional', 'Casual', 'Friendly' — tell AI how to sound.
content.note: 💡 Try answering: Who? What? How? How much? — in your prompt.

exercise.title: Practice
exercise.description: Send this specific prompt and see the difference:
exercise.defaultPrompt: Give me exactly 5 tips for learning Python programming — for complete beginners, each tip in 2 sentences with a practical example.

quiz.question: Why should you be specific in prompts?
options: AI gets confused by long text

quiz.question: Which makes a prompt more specific?

content.heading: Giving AI a Role (Persona)
content.body:
When you assign AI a specific role, it responds from that perspective.

**Formula:**
`You are a [expert/role]. [Your question.]`

**Why it works:**
AI adjusts its vocabulary, depth, and style based on the role you give it.

examples.label: No Role
examples.text: How do I start a business?

examples.label: With Role
examples.text: You are a 15-year experienced entrepreneur. I just graduated college with only $500. What are 3 practical small businesses I can start this week?

content.body2:
**Useful roles to try:**
- 'You are an experienced teacher'
- 'You are a senior software engineer'
- 'You are a financial advisor'
- 'You are a creative writing coach'
- 'You are a strict editor who values clarity'
content.note: 💡 Roles make responses more focused, relevant, and expert-level.

exercise.title: Try it
exercise.description: Ask AI something while assigning it a role:
exercise.defaultPrompt: You are an experienced resume expert. I'm a fresh graduate with no work experience. What should I include in my resume to stand out?

quiz.question: What's the benefit of assigning a role to AI?
options: It focuses the response from that expert perspective

quiz.question: What is the correct formula for role assignment?
options: Ask a question first, then add role
Just mention the role at the end

content.heading: Context — Help AI Understand Your Situation
content.body:
AI doesn't know you personally. The more context you provide, the better it can help.

**Context to include:**
- Who you are / what you do
- Your goal or objective
- Current problem or challenge
- What kind of answer you want
- Any constraints (time, budget, skill level)

examples.label: With Context
examples.text: I'm a medical student taking IELTS in 6 months. My current score is 5.5, and I'm weakest in Writing Task 2. I can study 30 minutes daily. Give me a structured improvement plan.

content.note: 💡 More context = more useful, personalized responses.

exercise.title: Write with your own context
exercise.description: Write a prompt with your real situation:
exercise.defaultPrompt: I am a university student studying Computer Science. I need to learn web development for an internship in 3 months. I know basic HTML/CSS. Give me a step-by-step learning roadmap.

quiz.question: What should you include for better context?

content.heading: Specify How You Want the Output
content.body:
AI can respond in many formats. Telling it what you want makes the response directly usable.

**Common formats:**
- **List:** 'Give it as numbered bullet points'
- **Table:** 'Create a comparison table with columns for X, Y, Z'
- **Steps:** 'Explain step by step'
- **Summary:** 'Summarize in exactly 3 sentences'
- **JSON:** 'Respond in JSON format'
- **Email:** 'Write it as a professional email'

examples.label: Table Format
examples.text: Compare Python, JavaScript, and Java in a table with 4 columns: Language, Best For, Learning Difficulty (1-10), Average Salary.

examples.label: Step Format
examples.text: Explain how to create a GitHub repository, step by step, for a complete beginner who has never used Git.

content.note: 💡 Specifying format makes the response immediately usable without editing.

exercise.title: Try a specific format
exercise.description: Create a comparison table:
exercise.defaultPrompt: Compare three popular AI chatbots (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini) in a table with columns: Name, Best Use Case, Free Tier, Strengths, Weaknesses.

quiz.question: Which is a valid output format request?
Why specify output format?
options: To make the response immediately usable

content.heading: Prompt Engineering: Do's and Don'ts
content.body:
**DO's ✅**
- Be clear and specific about what you want
- Provide relevant context and background
- Specify the output format you need
- Assign a role when appropriate
- Break complex tasks into smaller steps
- Use examples to show what you want

**DON'Ts ❌**
- Don't be vague or assume AI knows your situation
- Don't ask multiple unrelated questions in one prompt
- Don't forget to specify the audience or tone
- Don't accept the first response if it's not quite right
- Don't use ambiguous words like 'good', 'nice', 'better'
- Don't write prompts longer than necessary
content.note: 💡 If AI gives a wrong answer, it's usually a prompt problem — not an AI problem.

exercise.title: Fix this bad prompt
exercise.description: Rewrite this weak prompt to make it strong:
exercise.defaultPrompt: Write something good about technology for my presentation.

quiz.question: What should you do if AI gives a wrong answer?
options: Refine your prompt and try again

quiz.question: Which is a DON'T in prompt engineering?
options: Asking multiple unrelated questions at once

modules.title: Module 3: Intermediate Prompting
modules.description: Level up with advanced prompting strategies

content.heading: Chain of Thought — Make AI Think Step by Step
content.body:
**Chain of Thought (CoT)** is a technique where you ask AI to show its reasoning process before giving the final answer.

**Why it works:**
When AI 'thinks out loud', it catches its own mistakes and produces more accurate answers — especially for complex problems.

**How to use it:**
Simply add phrases like:
- 'Think step by step'
- 'Show your reasoning'
- 'Walk me through your thinking'
- 'Explain how you arrived at this answer'

examples.label: Without CoT
examples.text: If a train travels 120km in 2 hours, then 180km in 3 hours, what is the average speed?

examples.label: With CoT
examples.text: If a train travels 120km in 2 hours, then 180km in 3 hours, what is the average speed? Think step by step and show all calculations.

content.note: 💡 Chain of Thought is especially powerful for math, logic, and complex reasoning tasks.

exercise.title: Try Chain of Thought
exercise.description: Ask a reasoning question with step-by-step instruction:
exercise.defaultPrompt: I have a business idea for a mobile app. Think step by step and help me analyze: Is it a good idea? Consider market demand, competition, technical feasibility, and monetization.

quiz.question: What does Chain of Thought prompting do?
options: Makes AI show its reasoning process

quiz.question: When is Chain of Thought most useful?

content.heading: Few-Shot Prompting — Teach by Example
content.body:
**Few-shot prompting** means giving AI examples of what you want before making your actual request.

**Types:**
- **Zero-shot:** No examples, just instructions
- **One-shot:** One example given
- **Few-shot:** 2-5 examples given

**Why use examples?**
Examples show AI the exact pattern, tone, and format you want — much more powerful than describing it in words.

examples.label: Zero-shot (vague)
examples.text: Write product descriptions for my store.

examples.label: Few-shot (with examples)
examples.text:
Write product descriptions in this style:

Example 1:
Product: Coffee Mug
Description: Start your morning right. This ceramic mug keeps your coffee hot for 2 hours.

Example 2:
Product: Notebook
Description: Your ideas deserve better. Premium paper, lay-flat binding, built to last.

Now write a description for: Wireless Headphones

content.note: 💡 The more relevant examples you give, the more accurate the output pattern.

exercise.title: Try Few-Shot
exercise.description: Give examples and let AI follow the pattern:
exercise.defaultPrompt:
Classify these reviews as Positive or Negative:

Example: 'Amazing product, works perfectly!' → Positive
Example: 'Broke after one day, terrible quality' → Negative

Now classify: 'Decent quality but arrived late and packaging was damaged.'

quiz.question: What is few-shot prompting?
options: Giving AI examples before the actual request

quiz.question: What is zero-shot prompting?

content.heading: Iteration — Refine Until It's Perfect
content.body:
Getting the perfect response is rarely a one-shot process. Professional prompt engineers iterate — they refine their prompts based on outputs.

**Effective follow-up techniques:**

**Make it simpler:**
'Explain this in simpler language, like I'm 12 years old'

**Go deeper:**
'Expand on point number 3 with more detail and examples'

**Change format:**
'Convert this to a bullet point list'

**Adjust tone:**
'Rewrite this in a more formal/casual tone'

**Fix mistakes:**
'That's not quite right — [correct info]. Please rewrite with this in mind.'

**Shorten:**
'Summarize this in exactly 3 sentences'
content.note: 💡 Great prompt engineering is a conversation, not a single message.

exercise.title: Practice Iteration
exercise.description: First run the prompt, then try a follow-up:
exercise.defaultPrompt: Explain machine learning in simple terms.

quiz.question: What is prompt iteration?
options: Refining prompts based on AI output

content.heading: Controlling AI Creativity
content.body:
**Temperature** is a setting that controls how creative or conservative AI responses are.

**Low temperature (precise):**
- More predictable, factual, consistent
- Best for: coding, math, data extraction, factual Q&A

**High temperature (creative):**
- More varied, creative, surprising
- Best for: brainstorming, storytelling, creative writing

**You can control this in your prompt without the setting:**

For precise responses, say:
'Give me a factual, accurate answer with no speculation.'

For creative responses, say:
'Be creative, think outside the box, give unexpected ideas.'
content.note: 💡 Match the creativity level to your task — facts need precision, ideas need creativity.

exercise.title: Try both modes
exercise.description: Ask for creative vs factual responses:
exercise.defaultPrompt: Give me 10 unique business ideas for a student with no capital. Be extremely creative and think completely outside the box — the more unusual the better.

quiz.question: When should you want low temperature (precise) responses?

content.heading: Prompt Chaining — Break Big Tasks Down
content.body:
**Prompt chaining** means breaking a large complex task into smaller sequential prompts, where each builds on the last.

**Why it works:**
AI focuses better on smaller tasks. Chaining ensures each step is done well before moving forward.

**Example — Writing a Business Plan:**

Step 1: 'Generate 5 business ideas for the food industry'

Step 2: 'Take idea #3. What are the top 5 risks?'

Step 3: 'Now write an executive summary for this business'

Step 4: 'Create a 6-month action plan with milestones'
content.note: 💡 Complex task = chain of small prompts. Each response becomes context for the next.

exercise.title: Try Chaining
exercise.description: Start the first step of a chain:
exercise.defaultPrompt: I want to start a YouTube channel about technology. Step 1: Generate 10 unique channel name ideas that are catchy, memorable, and available as a username.

quiz.question: What is prompt chaining?
options: Breaking tasks into sequential smaller prompts

content.heading: System Prompts — Setting AI's Behavior
content.body:
A **system prompt** is a hidden instruction that sets the overall behavior, personality, and rules for an AI before the conversation begins.

**What system prompts define:**
- AI's role and personality
- What topics to cover or avoid
- Response style and format
- Language and tone

**Example system prompt:**
'You are a helpful customer service agent for TechCorp. Only answer questions about our products. Always be polite and professional. If you don't know something, say so and offer to escalate.'

**You can simulate this in conversation:**
Start your message with 'For this entire conversation, you are...' and AI will maintain that behavior.
content.note: 💡 System prompts are how companies customize AI for their specific use cases.

exercise.title: Create a System Prompt
exercise.description: Set AI's behavior for a specific use case:
exercise.defaultPrompt: For this entire conversation, you are a strict but encouraging coding teacher. Only speak about programming concepts. When I make mistakes, correct them firmly but kindly. Start by asking what I want to learn today.

quiz.question: What does a system prompt do?
options: Sets AI's overall behavior and personality

content.heading: ReAct Prompting — Reason + Act
content.body:
**ReAct** stands for **Reasoning + Acting**. It's a technique where you ask AI to alternate between thinking (reasoning) and doing (acting).

**The pattern:**
1. **Thought:** AI reasons about what to do
2. **Action:** AI takes a step
3. **Observation:** AI notes what happened
4. **Repeat** until task is complete

**How to prompt for ReAct:**
'For each step, first write your Thought (reasoning), then your Action (what you'll do), then your Observation (what you learned).'

**Best used for:**
- Complex problem solving
- Multi-step research tasks
- Debugging code
- Planning complex projects
content.note: 💡 ReAct helps AI solve problems more systematically, like a detective building a case.

exercise.title: Try ReAct
exercise.description: Ask AI to reason through a problem:
exercise.defaultPrompt: Use ReAct format (Thought/Action/Observation) to help me plan how to start a freelance graphic design business. Show your reasoning at each step.

quiz.question: What does ReAct stand for?

content.heading: Self-Consistency — Get Multiple Answers
content.body:
**Self-consistency** is a technique where you ask AI to solve the same problem multiple times using different approaches, then pick the most common answer.

**Why it works:**
Just like asking multiple experts and taking the majority opinion, multiple reasoning paths reduce errors.

**How to use it:**
'Solve this problem in 3 different ways. Then tell me which answer appears most consistently.'

**Best for:**
- Important decisions
- Complex math or logic
- When accuracy matters most
- Fact-checking important information
content.note: 💡 More reasoning paths = higher confidence in the answer.

exercise.title: Try Self-Consistency
exercise.description: Ask AI to approach a problem from multiple angles:
exercise.defaultPrompt: Should I learn Python or JavaScript first as a beginner programmer? Reason through this question from 3 different perspectives: career opportunities, ease of learning, and practical projects. Then give your final recommendation.

quiz.question: What is self-consistency in prompting?
options: AI always giving the same answer
Solving a problem multiple ways and finding the consistent answer

content.heading: Tree of Thought — Explore Multiple Paths
content.body:
**Tree of Thought (ToT)** extends Chain of Thought by exploring multiple reasoning branches simultaneously — like a decision tree.

**How it works:**
Instead of one linear reasoning path, AI explores several branches and evaluates which path leads to the best solution.

**Prompt template:**
'Imagine 3 different experts are solving this problem. Each expert writes their first step. Then all experts review each other's steps. Continue this process until the best solution emerges.'

**Best for:**
- Creative problem solving
- Strategic planning
- Complex decisions with many variables
- Situations where multiple solutions are possible
content.note: 💡 Tree of Thought works best for problems where the path to the answer matters as much as the answer itself.

exercise.title: Try Tree of Thought
exercise.description: Explore multiple solution paths:
exercise.defaultPrompt: Three experts (a marketing expert, a tech expert, and a finance expert) are evaluating my idea to build a food delivery app in Bangladesh. Each expert shares their biggest concern. Then they discuss solutions. Show this entire conversation.

quiz.question: How is Tree of Thought different from Chain of Thought?
options: It explores multiple reasoning branches simultaneously

content.heading: AI Ethics & Knowing the Limits
content.body:
Being a skilled prompt engineer also means understanding AI's ethical boundaries and limitations.

**AI Limitations:**
- Can hallucinate (generate false information confidently)
- Has a training cutoff date
- Can be biased based on training data
- Cannot access real-time information
- Cannot verify its own accuracy

**Ethical Considerations:**
- Don't use AI to generate harmful, misleading, or illegal content
- Always verify important information from authoritative sources
- Don't use AI to plagiarize or misrepresent work as your own
- Be transparent when content is AI-generated

**How to reduce hallucinations:**
- Ask AI to cite sources or admit uncertainty
- Use phrases like 'If you're not sure, say so'
- Verify critical information independently
content.note: 💡 A skilled AI user knows when to trust AI and when to verify independently.

exercise.title: Test AI Honesty
exercise.description: Ask AI to acknowledge its limitations:
exercise.defaultPrompt: What is the current population of Bangladesh? If you're not 100% sure of the exact current figure, please say so and explain your uncertainty. Also tell me when your training data ends.

quiz.question: What is AI 'hallucination'?
How can you reduce AI hallucinations?

content.heading: Understanding Jailbreaks & Staying Safe
content.body:
**What is a jailbreak?**
A jailbreak is an attempt to bypass AI's safety guidelines through clever prompting — getting AI to do something it's designed to refuse.

**Why AI has guardrails:**
AI companies add safety guidelines to prevent harmful content generation. These protect users and society.

**Why you should NOT jailbreak:**
- Violates terms of service
- Can expose you to harmful content
- Creates misinformation risks
- May have legal consequences

**Instead, use these legitimate techniques:**
- Frame requests constructively
- Provide clear legitimate context
- If AI refuses, rephrase — don't try to trick it
- Use the right AI tool for the right task
content.note: 💡 The best prompt engineers work with AI's design, not against it.

exercise.title: Legitimate Reframing
exercise.description: Practice rephrasing a sensitive request legitimately:
exercise.defaultPrompt: I'm writing a cybersecurity awareness article for my company. Explain the most common types of phishing attacks so employees can recognize and avoid them. Focus on awareness and protection.

quiz.question: What is an AI jailbreak?
options: Bypassing AI safety guidelines through prompting

modules.title: Module 5: Real-World Use Cases
modules.description: Apply AI to real tasks that matter

content.heading: Using AI to Write a Standout CV
content.body:
AI can dramatically improve your CV and cover letters — but only if you prompt it correctly.

**Step 1: Give AI your information**
List your education, experience, skills, and target job.

**Step 2: Specify the job**
Paste the job description so AI can tailor your CV.

**Step 3: Request specific improvements**
Ask AI to use action verbs, quantify achievements, and match keywords.

**Power verbs to request:**
Achieved, Delivered, Led, Built, Increased, Reduced, Managed, Developed
content.note: 💡 Always review AI-generated CVs carefully — personalize and verify every claim.

exercise.title: Try CV Writing
exercise.description: Use AI to improve a CV bullet point:
exercise.defaultPrompt:
You are an expert CV writer. Improve these weak CV bullet points using strong action verbs and quantified achievements:

1. 'Helped with social media'
2. 'Did customer service'
3. 'Worked on website'

Rewrite each one to be impressive and specific.

quiz.question: What should you provide AI to write a tailored CV?
options: Your information AND the job description

content.heading: Using AI to Build a Business Plan
content.body:
AI can help you create comprehensive business plans in a fraction of the time.

**Use AI for:**
- Market research and competitor analysis
- SWOT analysis
- Financial projections
- Marketing strategy
- Go-to-market planning

**Best prompting approach:**
Use prompt chaining — one section at a time:

1. Executive Summary
2. Market Analysis
3. Product/Service Description
4. Marketing Strategy
5. Financial Projections
6. Operations Plan
content.note: 💡 Use AI as a thinking partner for business planning, not as the final authority. Always validate with real data.

exercise.title: Business Plan Exercise
exercise.description: Start building a business plan section:
exercise.defaultPrompt: I want to start a tutoring service for HSC students in Dhaka, Bangladesh. Do a SWOT analysis for this business idea. Be specific to the Bangladeshi market context.

quiz.question: What is the best approach for using AI for business planning?
options: Ask for everything in one prompt
Use prompt chaining, one section at a time

content.heading: Using AI as a Coding Partner
content.body:
AI is one of the most powerful coding tools available — if you know how to prompt it.

**What AI can help with:**
- Writing code from scratch
- Debugging errors
- Explaining code
- Refactoring and optimization
- Writing documentation
- Code review

**Best practices for coding prompts:**
- Specify the programming language
- Describe the exact problem or feature
- Share relevant existing code
- Ask for explanations alongside code
- Request error handling and edge cases

examples.label: Strong
examples.text: Write a Python FastAPI endpoint for user login that accepts email and password, validates against a SQLite database using SQLAlchemy, returns a JWT token on success, and returns appropriate error messages on failure. Include comments explaining each step.

content.note: 💡 Always review and test AI-generated code before using it in production.

exercise.title: Coding Exercise
exercise.description: Ask AI to write specific code:
exercise.defaultPrompt: Write a Python function that takes a list of student names and scores, and returns a dictionary with 'passed' and 'failed' lists (passing score is 50). Include docstring and handle edge cases. Show example usage.

quiz.question: What should you include when asking AI to write code?
options: Programming language, exact requirements, and context

content.heading: Using AI for Content & Copywriting
content.body:
AI can dramatically speed up content creation — from blog posts to social media captions.

**Content types AI excels at:**
- Blog posts and articles
- Social media captions
- Product descriptions
- Email newsletters
- Ad copy
- Video scripts

**Key prompting tips for content:**
- Define your target audience clearly
- Specify tone (professional, casual, humorous)
- Give the word count or length
- Provide key points to include
- Share your brand voice if you have one
content.note: 💡 Use AI as a first draft tool — always add your personal voice and edit the output.

exercise.title: Content Writing Exercise
exercise.description: Write a social media post with AI:
exercise.defaultPrompt: Write 3 LinkedIn post options about the importance of learning AI skills in 2025. Target audience: fresh graduates in Bangladesh. Tone: inspiring and practical. Each post should be under 150 words and end with a call to action.

quiz.question: What is AI best used for in content writing?
options: Final published content with no edits
First drafts that you then refine

content.heading: Using AI for Research & Summarization
content.body:
AI can be a powerful research assistant — helping you understand complex topics quickly.

**Research tasks AI helps with:**
- Explaining complex concepts simply
- Summarizing long documents
- Comparing multiple perspectives
- Generating research questions
- Creating literature review outlines

**Summarization prompt formula:**
'Summarize [topic/text] in [format] for [audience] in [length]. Focus on [key aspects].'

**Important warning:**
AI may not have the latest information and can make factual errors. Always verify important facts from authoritative sources.
content.note: 💡 Use AI to understand concepts, but cite original sources in academic work.

exercise.title: Research Exercise
exercise.description: Use AI to research a topic:
exercise.defaultPrompt: I'm writing a university essay on climate change. Summarize the 5 most important scientific facts about climate change for a non-expert audience. Present as numbered points, each with a one-sentence explanation. Note any areas of scientific uncertainty.

quiz.question: What should you always do with AI research output?
options: Verify important facts from authoritative sources

content.heading: Using AI to Write Better Emails
content.body:
AI can help you write clearer, more professional, and more effective emails.

**Email types AI helps with:**
- Professional cold emails
- Follow-up emails
- Complaint responses
- Job application emails
- Apology emails
- Negotiation emails

**Prompt formula for emails:**
'Write a [tone] email to [recipient] about [topic]. Context: [situation]. Goal: [what you want to achieve]. Length: [short/medium/long]. Include [specific elements].'

**Always specify:**
- The relationship (boss, client, friend)
- The desired outcome
- The tone (formal, friendly, urgent)
content.note: 💡 AI-written emails should sound like you — always personalize before sending.

exercise.title: Email Writing Exercise
exercise.description: Write a professional email with AI:
exercise.defaultPrompt: Write a professional email requesting a 1-week deadline extension for a project. Context: I'm a freelancer, the client is understanding but professional. Reason: unexpected illness. Tone: apologetic but confident. Include: acknowledgment of the inconvenience, assurance of quality, new deadline proposal.

quiz.question: What should you always do with AI-written emails?

content.heading: Using AI for Data Analysis
content.body:
AI can help you understand, interpret, and present data — even without technical expertise.

**Data tasks AI helps with:**
- Interpreting data patterns
- Writing SQL queries
- Creating data analysis plans
- Suggesting visualizations
- Writing data-driven reports
- Explaining statistical concepts

**Prompt approach for data:**
Describe your data clearly, then ask specific questions:
'I have a dataset with columns: [list columns]. Here is a sample: [sample data]. Help me [specific task].'

**Tip:** Paste small data samples directly into your prompt for AI to analyze.
content.note: 💡 AI can't access your files directly — paste data samples into the prompt for analysis.

exercise.title: Data Analysis Exercise
exercise.description: Analyze sample data with AI:
exercise.defaultPrompt:
Analyze this sales data and identify key insights:

Month | Sales | Units | Region
Jan   | 45000 | 120   | North
Feb   | 38000 | 98    | North
Mar   | 62000 | 156   | South
Apr   | 71000 | 189   | South

What trends do you see? What would you recommend to the sales team?

quiz.question: How do you share data with AI for analysis?
options: Paste data samples into the prompt

modules.title: Module 6: AI Tools Ecosystem
modules.description: Navigate the AI landscape like a pro

content.heading: Choosing the Right AI Tool
content.body:
Not all AI tools are equal. Each has strengths and weaknesses.

**ChatGPT (OpenAI)**
- Best for: general tasks, plugins, image generation (DALL-E)
- Strengths: most popular, huge ecosystem, code interpreter
- Weaknesses: can be verbose, expensive at scale

**Claude (Anthropic)**
- Best for: long documents, nuanced writing, analysis
- Strengths: large context window, honest about limitations
- Weaknesses: less real-time data access

**Gemini (Google)**
- Best for: Google Workspace integration, real-time info
- Strengths: connected to Google Search, free tier
- Weaknesses: less consistent than competitors

**Rule of thumb:**
Use the tool that fits your specific task — don't be loyal to one.
content.note: 💡 Professional AI users switch between tools based on the task requirements.

exercise.title: Compare AI Tools
exercise.description: Ask AI to help you choose:
exercise.defaultPrompt: I'm a freelance content writer. I need to choose between ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini for my daily work. Compare them specifically for content writing tasks. Which should I use for which type of content?

quiz.question: Which AI is known for its large context window and honest limitations?

content.heading: AI Image Generation — Prompting Visuals
content.body:
Image AI tools can create stunning visuals from text descriptions. Prompting for images is a skill in itself.

**Popular image AI tools:**
- **Midjourney** — highest quality, subscription-based
- **DALL-E 3** — integrated with ChatGPT
- **Stable Diffusion** — free, open source
- **Adobe Firefly** — commercial-safe images

**Image prompt formula:**
[Subject] + [Style] + [Lighting] + [Mood] + [Technical specs]

**Example:**
'A young Bangladeshi entrepreneur at a laptop in a modern cafe, photorealistic, warm morning light, motivated and focused, 4K, cinematic'
content.note: 💡 More detail in image prompts = more accurate visual output.

exercise.title: Write an Image Prompt
exercise.description: Practice writing a detailed image prompt:
exercise.defaultPrompt: Help me write a detailed image generation prompt for: A futuristic classroom in Bangladesh where students are learning with AI. Use the formula: Subject + Style + Lighting + Mood + Technical specs. Give me 3 variations.

quiz.question: What makes a good image generation prompt?
options: Detailed subject, style, lighting, mood, and specs

content.heading: Automating Work with AI Tools
content.body:
AI automation tools let you connect apps and create automated workflows without coding.

**Popular automation platforms:**
- **Zapier** — connects 5000+ apps with AI
- **Make (Integromat)** — visual workflow builder
- **n8n** — open source automation

**Example automations:**
- When you get an email → AI summarizes it → sends to Slack
- When a form is submitted → AI writes a personalized response
- Daily → AI generates a social media post and schedules it

**No-code AI tools:**
- **Notion AI** — smart notes and documents
- **Grammarly** — AI writing assistant
- **Otter.ai** — AI meeting transcription
content.note: 💡 AI automation can save hours of repetitive work every week.

exercise.title: Design an Automation
exercise.description: Use AI to plan an automation:
exercise.defaultPrompt: I run a small online store. Help me design an AI automation workflow that: takes customer reviews → analyzes sentiment → drafts a personalized response → flags negative reviews for human review. Describe each step and what tool could handle it.

quiz.question: What do AI automation tools like Zapier help with?
options: Connecting apps and creating automated workflows

content.heading: AI APIs — Integrating AI into Your Products
content.body:
An **API (Application Programming Interface)** lets you integrate AI capabilities directly into your own applications.

**Why use APIs:**
- Build custom AI-powered products
- Automate at scale
- Create unique user experiences
- Control costs more precisely

**Popular AI APIs:**
- **Anthropic API** — Claude models
- **OpenAI API** — GPT models
- **Google AI API** — Gemini models
- **Hugging Face** — open source models

**A simple API call:**
You send: your prompt + API key
You receive: AI's response as text

This is exactly how PromptForge Academy's AI grader works!
content.note: 💡 You don't need to be an expert programmer to start using AI APIs — many tutorials exist for beginners.

exercise.title: API Concepts
exercise.description: Learn about APIs through AI:
exercise.defaultPrompt: Explain how to use the Claude API as if teaching a beginner programmer. What do I need to get started? What does a basic API call look like? Keep it simple with a Python code example.

quiz.question: What does an AI API allow you to do?
options: Integrate AI into your own applications

content.heading: The Future of AI — What's Coming
content.body:
AI is evolving at an unprecedented pace. Here's what's on the horizon:

**Near-term trends (1-2 years):**
- Multimodal AI (text + image + audio + video together)
- AI agents that take actions autonomously
- More personalized AI assistants
- AI in every productivity app

**Medium-term (3-5 years):**
- AI that can independently conduct research
- More accurate and reliable models
- Widespread AI regulation
- New job categories centered around AI

**What this means for you:**
- Prompt engineering will become a foundational skill
- AI literacy will be as important as computer literacy
- Human creativity + AI capability = the winning combination
- Learning to work with AI now gives you a massive advantage
content.note: 💡 The best time to learn AI was yesterday. The second best time is today.

exercise.title: Future Thinking
exercise.description: Explore the future of AI:
exercise.defaultPrompt: How will AI change the job market in Bangladesh over the next 5 years? Which industries will be most affected? What skills should young Bangladeshis develop today to stay relevant? Be specific and realistic.

quiz.question: What is 'multimodal AI'?
options: AI that handles text, image, audio, and video together

modules.title: Module 7: Capstone Projects
modules.description: Apply everything you've learned in real projects

content.heading: Project 1 — Design Your Personal AI Assistant
content.body:
In this project, you'll design a complete personal AI assistant using everything you've learned.

**Your mission:**
Create a system prompt + prompt library for a personal AI assistant tailored to your specific life and work.

**Step 1: Define your assistant's purpose**
What will it help you with daily? (studying, writing, coding, business?)

**Step 2: Write the system prompt**
Describe its personality, expertise, and behavior rules.

**Step 3: Create 10 power prompts**
Write 10 prompts you'll use daily with this assistant.

**Step 4: Test and refine**
Test each prompt, iterate based on results.
content.note: 💡 A well-designed personal AI assistant can save you 2-3 hours every day.

exercise.title: Build Your Assistant
exercise.description: Start by designing your system prompt:
exercise.defaultPrompt: I want to build a personal AI assistant for a university student studying Computer Science. Help me write a detailed system prompt that makes the AI: act as a study partner, help with coding, explain concepts simply, keep me accountable, and encourage me when I'm stuck. The assistant should be friendly but focused.

quiz.question: What is the first step in building a personal AI assistant?

content.heading: Project 2 — Validate a Business Idea with AI
content.body:
Use AI to do a complete business validation analysis for a real idea you have.

**The full validation chain:**

**Prompt 1:** Generate and refine the business concept
**Prompt 2:** Identify target market and customer persona
**Prompt 3:** Analyze 5 competitors
**Prompt 4:** SWOT analysis
**Prompt 5:** Revenue model options
**Prompt 6:** First 30-day action plan
**Prompt 7:** Key risks and mitigation strategies

**Deliverable:**
A complete business validation report created through 7 chained prompts.
content.note: 💡 This project demonstrates the full power of prompt chaining for complex, real-world tasks.

exercise.title: Start Validation
exercise.description: Begin the business validation chain:
exercise.defaultPrompt: I have a business idea: an online platform connecting university students with part-time job opportunities in Bangladesh. Start the validation by: 1) Refining and improving the concept, 2) Identifying the primary target market with a detailed customer persona, 3) Estimating market size in Bangladesh.

quiz.question: What prompting technique does this project primarily use?

content.heading: Project 3 — Build Your Personal Prompt Library
content.body:
A prompt library is a collection of tested, refined prompts for tasks you do regularly.

**Why build a prompt library:**
- Save time on repeated tasks
- Ensure consistent quality
- Build on what works
- Share with your team

**Categories to include:**
- Work/Study prompts
- Writing & editing prompts
- Research prompts
- Creative prompts
- Analysis prompts
- Personal productivity prompts

**Library format:**
[Task Name] | [Prompt] | [Best Model] | [Notes]

**Goal:** Create 20 high-quality prompts across 5 categories.
content.note: 💡 Your prompt library becomes more valuable over time as you refine and add to it.

exercise.title: Start Your Library
exercise.description: Create your first prompt library category:
exercise.defaultPrompt: Help me build a 'Writing & Editing' section of my prompt library. Create 5 high-quality prompts for: 1) Making writing more concise, 2) Improving clarity, 3) Checking grammar and style, 4) Making text more engaging, 5) Adapting writing for different audiences. Format as a table.

quiz.question: What is a prompt library?
options: A collection of tested prompts for regular tasks

content.heading: Final Assessment — Prove Your Skills
content.body:
Congratulations on reaching the final lesson! 🎉

**What you've mastered:**
- ✅ AI fundamentals and how LLMs work
- ✅ Basic prompting: specificity, roles, context, format
- ✅ Intermediate: Chain of Thought, Few-shot, Iteration
- ✅ Advanced: System prompts, ReAct, Tree of Thought
- ✅ Real-world applications across multiple domains
- ✅ AI tools ecosystem and future trends
- ✅ Three complete capstone projects

**Your final challenge:**
Combine ALL techniques learned into one master prompt that solves a real problem you face.

**Certificate:** Complete this lesson to unlock your PromptForge Academy certificate!
content.note: 💡 You've gone from beginner to professional prompt engineer. The journey continues — AI keeps evolving, and so should you.

exercise.title: Master Challenge
exercise.description: Create your ultimate prompt combining all techniques:
exercise.defaultPrompt: Using ALL prompt engineering techniques (role, context, chain of thought, few-shot, output format, iteration instructions), write the most powerful prompt you can for this task: Help me prepare for a job interview at a top tech company in Bangladesh. I'm a fresh Computer Science graduate.

quiz.question: How many modules does PromptForge Academy have?
What do you receive after completing all lessons?
